observations.text: The message asks the recipient to update payroll direct deposit and includes highly sensitive values in the paste.

evidence.text: An SSN-like value, password, and one-time code appear in the pasted text.

inferences.text: Sensitive secrets in the paste create privacy risk if sent to a model provider; the content may also be a credential-harvesting lure.

unknowns.text: Whether HR actually sent this request was not verified.

confidence.rationale: Privacy-sensitive content should be redacted before live analysis whenever possible.

recommended_next_steps.text: Remove SSN, passwords, and one-time codes before any further analysis or sharing.
Confirm payroll changes only through an official HR channel you already trust.
